refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) for operational use, ensuring data acquisition, processing, and dissemination meet latency targets.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly address the key functional steps impacting latency: data acquisition, processing, and dissemination. This clarifies verification and design focus areas.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency from data capture to model update availability; verify p95 latency ≤ 1.0 s under representative operational scenarios.
sub_frs: Acquire real-time worksite data with minimal delay
Process and update the worksite model within latency constraints
Disseminate updated worksite model to relevant modules promptly

candidate_dps: High-bandwidth telemetry data streaming with edge processing
Centralized model update server with optimized algorithms
Priority network scheduling for worksite model dissemination
selected_dp.selected_dp: High-bandwidth telemetry data streaming with edge processing

potential_sources: Telemetry data dependency on Vehicle and Teleoperation modules
Processing tightly coupled with NGHE Solution module
coupling_check.notes: The DP logically separates data acquisition (Telemetry) and processing (NGHE Solution) with clear interface boundaries, minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network link supports required bandwidth and latency
Risk of telemetry data loss impacting update timeliness
Processing algorithms can meet latency targets under load
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor telemetry network performance and consider fallback mechanisms for data loss.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Responsible for processing and updating the worksite model using telemetry data streamed from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time worksite data streams from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data transmission between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and NGHE Solution modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: Primary processing and update logic contained here; minimal diffusion.
Data acquisition but no processing; high diffusion risk if processing logic placed here.
Similar to Vehicle; telemetry data source only.
Not involved in worksite model update.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing processing and update logic in NGHE Solution minimizes diffusion and supports latency requirements.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module is overloaded
Dependency on telemetry data quality and network link reliability
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Implement monitoring and load balancing within NGHE Solution; ensure robust telemetry error handling.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR clearly maps to the selected DP with explicit focus on telemetry streaming and edge processing to meet latency.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion as processing logic is centralized in NGHE Solution module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support NGHE Solution as the best ownership candidate.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring telemetry latency and model update times within NGHE Solution module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of telemetry interface and processing responsibilities to NGHE Solution.

recommended_revisions: Add fallback strategies for telemetry data loss.
Define monitoring metrics for network link performance.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment are consistent, with clear ownership and manageable risks. Verification approaches are feasible and aligned with stakeholder requirements.
next_level_sub_frs: Acquire real-time worksite data with minimal delay
Process and update the worksite model within latency constraints
Disseminate updated worksite model to relevant modules promptly

revise.why: Current level is sufficiently detailed and consistent; no immediate revision needed.

assumptions: Network link supports required bandwidth and latency.
Telemetry data quality is sufficient for timely updates.
open_risks: Telemetry data loss impacting update timeliness.
Potential processing bottlenecks under peak load.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements from telemetry data streaming.
Performance benchmarks of NGHE Solution processing algorithms.
